skill.name: skill-creator
skill.description: Guide for creating and updating AI skills with structured instructions, reusable resources, validation scripts, and packaging workflows for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
skill.summary: Create, validate, and package reusable AI skills with clear structure and progressive disclosure.
skill.author: vercel-labs

security_audit.summary: AI review dismissed the static high and critical signals as false positives from Apache license text, Markdown examples, and validation strings. The skill does include executable Python scripts that write files and package entire directories, so publication is reasonable with a filesystem warning. No evidence found of prompt injection, credential exfiltration, network activity, or weak cryptography.

medium_findings.title: Initializer Writes to User-Supplied Paths
medium_findings.description: The initializer combines a user-provided output path and skill name, then creates directories and files. It does not validate the skill name before writing, so malformed names could write outside the intended location when the script is run manually.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The filesystem writes are direct and depend on command-line input. The risk requires a user to run the bundled script with unsafe arguments, so impact is moderate rather than high.

medium_findings.title: Packager Archives All Files Recursively
medium_findings.description: The packaging script walks the full skill directory and writes every file into the package. This is expected behavior, but users could accidentally include local secrets, build artifacts, or private files if they package the wrong directory.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The recursive archive behavior is clear from the code and has a realistic accidental disclosure risk. It is not malicious because the script only packages a path chosen by the user.

low_findings.title: False Positive: License URLs and Legal Text
low_findings.description: The hardcoded URLs are standard Apache License references. The weak cryptography detections point to legal terms and license wording, not cryptographic code or runtime behavior.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are license content and documentation URLs. They do not execute network requests or implement cryptography.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Examples Triggered Execution Patterns
low_findings.description: Many external command detections are Markdown fences, path examples, or inline filenames in instructional documentation. They describe skill design patterns and are not shell execution code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The evidence is inside Markdown examples and prose. No Ruby backtick execution or shell invocation is present in these cited locations.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Validator Uses Safe Parsing and Local Checks
low_findings.description: The validator reads SKILL.md, parses YAML with safe_load, and checks string patterns. The sensitive-file and reconnaissance detections do not indicate credential access or system inventory collection.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The script only reads a local skill file and validates frontmatter. It does not enumerate secrets, read key material, or send data anywhere.

dangerous_patterns.title: Path Construction from Unsanitized Arguments
dangerous_patterns.description: The initializer constructs output paths from command-line arguments before creating directories and files. Validate skill names before joining paths to reduce accidental or malicious path traversal.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The relevant input and path construction are visible. The finding depends on manual script execution, which limits exploitability.

dangerous_patterns.title: Unfiltered Recursive Archive Creation
dangerous_patterns.description: The packager includes every regular file found under the selected skill directory. Add exclusions or preflight warnings for secrets and generated files.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The recursive package behavior is explicit. The risk is accidental data inclusion rather than confirmed malicious exfiltration.

content.value_statement: Teams need consistent ways to package reusable agent knowledge and workflows. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through skill planning, authoring, validation, and packaging.
actual_capabilities: Explains the required SKILL.md structure and frontmatter fields.
Guides progressive disclosure across instructions, references, scripts, and assets.
Provides workflow patterns for planning reusable skill resources.
Includes a Python initializer that creates a starter skill directory.
Includes validation and packaging scripts for distributable .skill files.
Offers reference patterns for workflows and consistent output formats.
limitations: It does not decide the domain requirements without user examples or constraints.
Generated starter files are placeholders that require manual editing.
The packager includes all files under the selected skill directory.
It does not submit skills to any marketplace or registry.

use_cases.title: Build a New Team Skill
use_cases.description: Create a structured skill for a repeated internal workflow, including instructions, references, and optional scripts.

use_cases.title: Modernize an Existing Skill
use_cases.description: Review an existing skill, reduce unnecessary context, and move detailed material into reference files.

use_cases.title: Package a Skill for Sharing
use_cases.description: Validate a completed skill and produce a distributable package for another user or environment.

prompt_templates.title: Plan a Basic Skill
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me plan a new skill for this repeated task: [describe task]. Identify the required instructions, references, scripts, and assets.

prompt_templates.title: Create the Starter Structure
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a new skill named [skill-name] in [output path]. Use the skill creator workflow and explain what files I should customize next.

prompt_templates.title: Improve an Existing Skill
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this skill for clarity, progressive disclosure, and unnecessary files. Suggest focused edits that preserve its current behavior.

prompt_templates.title: Validate and Package
prompt_templates.prompt: Validate this completed skill, fix any structural issues, and package it for distribution. Flag any files that should not be included.

output_examples.input: I need a skill for repeated PDF form filling tasks.
output_examples.output: A proposed skill plan with a concise SKILL.md workflow, a form analysis script, a field mapping reference, and validation steps.

output_examples.input: This existing skill is too long and hard to maintain.
output_examples.output: A refactoring plan that keeps core instructions in SKILL.md and moves detailed examples into targeted reference files.

output_examples.input: Prepare this finished skill for sharing.
output_examples.output: A validation result, packaging steps, and a warning to remove private files before creating the distributable package.

best_practices: Start with concrete user examples before designing the skill structure.
Keep SKILL.md concise and move detailed material into clearly referenced files.
Run validation and inspect package contents before sharing a skill.
anti_patterns: Do not add broad documentation files that do not support the runtime workflow.
Do not store secrets, private datasets, or local credentials inside a skill directory.
Do not use scripts when concise instructions are enough for reliable execution.

faq.question: What does this skill help create?
faq.answer: It helps create AI skills that package instructions, references, scripts, and assets for repeated agent workflows.

faq.question: Can it update an existing skill?
faq.answer: Yes. It provides guidance for revising structure, reducing context, and improving reusable resources.

faq.question: Does it generate starter files?
faq.answer: Yes. The included initializer can create a starter directory with placeholder instructions, references, scripts, and assets.

faq.question: Does it validate skill packages?
faq.answer: Yes. The included validator checks basic SKILL.md frontmatter and naming requirements.

faq.question: Does it publish skills automatically?
faq.answer: No. It can package a skill file, but publication is handled outside this skill.

faq.question: What should users review before packaging?
faq.answer: Users should inspect the skill directory and remove secrets, private files, and unnecessary generated artifacts.
